title: nectaroscordum siculum subsp. bulgaricum
intro: Nectaroscordum siculum subsp. bulgaricum, also known as the Bulgarian honey garlic, is a bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Allium family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Its health benefits include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. The plant's bulbs can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in culinary dishes, to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
